methods.description: Process for measuring photosynthetic efficiency using a pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer to determine the fluorescence of PSII.

steps.description: Prior to measurement, the number of cells was equalized by adding a quantity of medium to achieve approximately 7700 cells mL −1.
Immediately after sampling, the algal suspension was acclimated in darkness for 5 min at 16°C and put in 5 mL Quartz cuvettes.
steps.searchable_summary: acclimate algal suspension in darkness at 16°C for 5 minutes

steps.description: The suspensions were gently stirred using a small magnetic bean during the fluorescence measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: stir suspensions with magnetic stirrer during measurements

steps.description: The maximum quantum yield (Fv/Fm) was measured at time zero (n = 6).
After dark incubation, a pulse of weak, far-red light was applied to empty the electron pool from QA.
steps.searchable_summary: apply far-red light pulse to empty electron pool

steps.description: The initial fluorescence (F0) was measured with red measuring light and the maximum fluorescence (Fm) was determined using a saturating white light pulse.
steps.searchable_summary: measure initial fluorescence F0 and maximum fluorescence Fm

methods.description: Procedure for measuring photosynthesis in terms of the relative electron transport rate (rETR) versus irradiance curves using a pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer.

steps.description: Photosynthesis (in terms of the relative electron transport rate, rETR = PFR · ∆F/Fm) versus irradiance curves were also measured.
Thirteen levels of light intensity from white light LED of the Water PAM, ranging from 5 to 1679 µmol photons m−2 s−1, were used to create the rETR curves, the duration of each intensity being 30 s.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 13 levels of light intensity to create rETR curves

steps.description: The hyperbolic tangent model of Jassby and Platt (1976) was used to estimate rETR curve parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: apply hyperbolic tangent model to estimate rETR parameters

steps.description: The saturation irradiance for electron transport (Ik) was calculated as the light intensity at which the initial slope of the curve (α) intercepts the horizontal asymptote (rETRmax).
The curve fit was calculated with the Solver Module of MS-Excel using the least squares method and comparing differences between measured and calculated data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform curve fitting using least squares method in Excel
